title: Deep Bayesian active learning using in-memory computing hardware
methods: in situ Gaussian random number generation via Lindeberg-Feller CLT
smooth transition weight update ratio annealing
Bayesian KL-divergence loss with expected log-likelihood
data_sources: Custom ESCIM memristor chip (4K array, TiN/TaOx/HfOx/TiN 1T1R)
core_conclusion: A memristor-based computation-in-memory (CIM) system natively executes deep Bayesian active learning by exploiting the intrinsic stochasticity of memristor devices to generate Gaussian random numbers in situ, eliminating the von Neumann bottleneck. The memristor stochastic gradient Langevin dynamics (mSGLD) method enables a four-layer Bayesian deep neural network to accurately capture parameter uncertainty and select maximally informative samples. On a robot pouring skill-learning task, the system achieves a 44% speed boost and 153x energy savings versus CMOS hardware, using as few as 128 labeled samples total.
why_it_matters: First hardware-native implementation of deep Bayesian active learning at the edge; converts device noise from a defect into a computational asset for probabilistic inference; demonstrates 153x energy savings over CMOS for the same DBAL task; validates the full active-learning loop on a real memristor chip with a practical robot benchmark; opens a hardware pathway for trustworthy, calibrated AI in label-scarce domains.
why_nature: Hardware-algorithm co-design bridging memristor physics, Bayesian statistics, and active learning — a genuinely cross-disciplinary contribution; validated on custom 4K memristor chip meeting Nature Computational Science's bar for experimental demonstration; domain-agnostic paradigm (robotics, chemistry, drug discovery); concrete 153x energy-efficiency figure with policy relevance; open data and code on Zenodo and GitHub.
added_value_to_my_research: Analogue Bayesian RL on neuromorphic chip -> uncertainty-aware sequential site-selection for facility-location optimization (EV charging, healthcare access)
mSGLD uncertainty sampling + sketch compression -> active labeling strategy for geospatial foundation model fine-tuning with minimal annotation cost
Straddle uncertainty criterion -> active spatial sampling framework for 2SFCA demand-surface estimation, prioritizing which census units to label first
Fully on-chip Bayesian learning (sign-gradient rule) -> privacy-preserving, federated active learning of spatial health patterns from mobile health devices
mSGLD + physics-infused graph neural network -> hardware-native active GeoAI pipeline where spatial autocorrelation priors guide which POI or mobility edges to observe next

related.title: Fully analogue reinforcement learning with memristors
related.relation: extends paper's memristor DBAL to analogue RL on the same hardware class

related.title: Random sketch learning for deep neural networks in edge computing
related.relation: sketch compression addresses the large-model scaling limitation of the paper's 4K memristor BDNN

related.title: Uncertainty-driven dynamics for active learning of interatomic potentials
related.relation: parallel uncertainty-driven active learning in a different scientific domain, validates the paradigm beyond robotics

related.title: Synaptic and neural behaviours in a standard silicon transistor
related.relation: synaptic plasticity in CMOS provides a path to on-chip local learning rules, addressing the paper's remaining digital-computer bottleneck for backpropagation

related.title: Deep learning accelerates discovery of complex nanomaterials
related.relation: physics-infused graph neural network for materials discovery — the paper's stated catalyst-discovery application domain, testbed for extending mSGLD beyond flat BDNNs

figures.caption: DBAL process overview, application domains (embodied learning, catalyst/drug discovery, protein optimization), and memristor-based neuromorphic hardware concept
Stochastic characteristics of memristors: I-V curve, read noise distribution (double exponential), analog switching under pulse trains, and conductance transition Gaussian distributions at 4K array

In-memory DBAL framework and mSGLD in situ learning: Gaussian weight realization in crossbar, DBAL flowchart, initial/latter mSGLD phases, weight update strategy, and uncertainty via multiple forward passes

Robot pouring skill learning results: training accuracy evolution (64-124 samples), conductance state distribution, active vs. passive learning accuracy gap (~13%), pouring performance, and decision-boundary visualization
